article_items.title: Perplexity launches AI-powered browser Comet, available via waitlist
article_items.description: Perplexity AI launches Comet, a Chromium-based browser with built-in AI and a Coinbase partnership for real-time crypto data. Here's what it means for traders.
article_items.excerpt: Perplexity AI launches Comet, a Chromium-based browser with built-in AI and a Coinbase partnership for real-time crypto data. Here's what it means for traders.
article_items.summary: The invite-only launch is restricted to Perplexity Max subscribers, who pay $200 per month for the privilege of being early. Just one day later, on July 10, Perplexity announced a partnership with Coinbase to pipe real-time crypto market data directly into the browser. Perplexity launches AI-powered browser Comet, available via waitlist The AI search company's new Chromium-based browser ships with a Coinbase partnership for real-time crypto data baked in from day one. Every Comet user who checks a token price through the browser is engaging with Coinbase’s data infrastructure, even if they never open the Coinbase app itself. The $200 monthly price tag for early access also raises questions about Comet’s eventual business model.
article_items.what_happened: The invite-only launch is restricted to Perplexity Max subscribers, who pay $200 per month for the privilege of being early. Just one day later, on July 10, Perplexity announced a partnership with Coinbase to pipe real-time crypto market data directly into the browser.

key_points: The invite-only launch is restricted to Perplexity Max subscribers, who pay $200 per month for the privilege of being early.
Just one day later, on July 10, Perplexity announced a partnership with Coinbase to pipe real-time crypto market data directly into the browser.
The $200 monthly price tag for early access also raises questions about Comet’s eventual business model.
Mobile versions are also in the pipeline, with an Android release slated for November 2025 and iOS following in March 2026.
Perplexity AI launches Comet, a Chromium-based browser with built-in AI and a Coinbase partnership for real-time crypto data.

Perplexity launches AI-powered browser Comet, available via waitlist The AI search company's new Chromium-based browser ships with a Coinbase partnership for real-time crypto data baked in from day one.
Perplexity plans to open Comet to the broader public on October 2, 2025, removing the paywall and making the browser freely available worldwide.

The difference is what sits on top: Perplexity’s conversational AI is embedded as the default search engine, turning the browser into something closer to an interactive research assistant than a traditional window to.
What Comet actually does Comet is built on the Chromium framework, the same open-source foundation that powers Chrome, Brave, Edge, and most of the browsers you’ve actually used.
Every Comet user who checks a token price through the browser is engaging with Coinbase’s data infrastructure, even if they never open the Coinbase app itself.
For Coinbase, the partnership extends its data distribution footprint beyond its own app and website.
Perplexity AI rolled out its Comet browser on July 9, 2025, giving its highest-tier subscribers a first look at what the company hopes will become a serious challenger to Google Chrome.

article_items.title: What the First iOS 27 Leaked Images Reveal About Your iPhone
article_items.description: Leaked images of iOS 27 reveal a massive Siri redesign integrated into the Dynamic Island, alongside new AI-powered camera widgets and photo editing tools.
article_items.excerpt: Leaked images of iOS 27 reveal a massive Siri redesign integrated into the Dynamic Island, alongside new AI-powered camera widgets and photo editing tools.
article_items.summary: Redesigned Siri: Smarter, Faster and More Integrated One of the standout features of iOS 27 is the complete redesign of Siri , Apple’s voice assistant. AI-Powered Features: Smarter Tools for Everyday Tasks Artificial intelligence takes center stage in iOS 27, introducing tools designed to simplify and enhance your daily interactions. Apple has addressed the bugs and performance issues that plagued iOS 26, making sure a smoother and more reliable user experience. Camera Customization: More Control at Your Fingertips The camera app in iOS 27 receives a significant overhaul, focusing on user control and customization . key_points: Redesigned Siri: Smarter, Faster and More Integrated One of the standout features of iOS 27 is the complete redesign of Siri , Apple’s voice assistant.
AI-Powered Features: Smarter Tools for Everyday Tasks Artificial intelligence takes center stage in iOS 27, introducing tools designed to simplify and enhance your daily interactions.
The video below from iDB gives us more details about the new iOS 27.

In the realm of photo editing, iOS 27 introduces advanced AI tools like “Extend” and “Reframe.” The “Extend” feature allows you to expand the dimensions of your photos seamlessly, while “Reframe” lets you adjust angles.
Leaked images of iOS 27 reveal a massive Siri redesign integrated into the Dynamic Island, alongside new AI-powered camera widgets and photo editing tools.
Additionally, iOS 27 introduces a dedicated Siri app , transforming the assistant into a chatbot-like tool.
Whether you’re snapping quick photos or working on a detailed photography project, iOS 27 enables you to make the most of your device’s camera capabilities.
Improved Stability and Performance While the new features are exciting, iOS 27 also focuses on improving system stability and performance .
Whether you’re drawn to the advanced AI tools, the intuitive multitasking capabilities, or the streamlined performance, iOS 27 is designed to adapt seamlessly to your needs.
Whether you’re a casual iPhone owner or a tech enthusiast, iOS 27 promises to address past performance concerns while introducing tools that redefine how you interact with your device.

article_items.title: OpenAI, SpaceX funding fuels bets on next-wave Asian AI winners
article_items.description: Wealthy Asian investors bet $24.3B on AI in 2025 as OpenAI's $122B round and SpaceX's IPO drive demand for Asian chipmakers and component suppliers.
article_items.excerpt: Wealthy Asian investors bet $24.3B on AI in 2025 as OpenAI's $122B round and SpaceX's IPO drive demand for Asian chipmakers and component suppliers.
article_items.summary: Wealthy Asian investors bet $24.3B on AI in 2025 as OpenAI's $122B round and SpaceX's IPO drive demand for Asian chipmakers and component suppliers. South Korean chipmakers Samsung and SK Hynix have reportedly secured supply agreements tied to OpenAI’s Stargate project, the ambitious infrastructure buildout designed to power the next generation of AI models. South Korean chipmakers Samsung and SK Hynix have reportedly secured supply agreements tied to OpenAI’s Stargate project, the ambitious infrastructure buildout designed to power the next generation of AI models.
OpenAI’s latest round valued the company at $852 billion post-money, with backing from Amazon, NVIDIA, and SoftBank.
When OpenAI closed its record $122 billion funding round on March 31, the biggest winners might not have been in San Francisco.
SpaceX has filed for what could become the largest IPO in history, targeting a valuation between $1.75 trillion and $2 trillion, with a listing anticipated around mid-June 2026.
The money trail from Silicon Valley to Asia In 2025, wealthy Asians poured $24.3 billion into global AI private funding rounds.
Asia’s own AI ecosystem is heating up AI startup funding across Asia hit a record $11.2 billion in the first quarter of 2026 alone, heavily concentrated in Chinese companies.
Why this matters for investors The risk, of course, is concentration.
That’s nearly triple what they invested the year before.

And the geopolitical overlay is impossible to ignore: trade tensions, export controls on advanced chips, and shifting alliances between the US and Asian manufacturing hubs add layers of uncertainty that pure financial.

OpenAI, SpaceX funding fuels bets on next-wave Asian AI winners Wealthy Asian investors are pouring billions into AI supply chain companies, betting that massive US fundraising rounds will lift the boats of Asian.
If AI spending plateaus or the Stargate project encounters delays, the same supply chain exposure that looks brilliant today could become a liability.

The company most recently raised $315 million in a Series E, featuring General Atlantic, AMD Ventures and Nvidia , resulting in a $5.3 billion valuation. Nvidia-backed $5 billion AI company tells CNBC of major London expansion AI company Runway is planning a major expansion in London, the company exclusively told CNBC on Monday. “London puts us close to many of our largest European customers already doing serious work with Runway, including BBC, Fremantle and WPP, and it builds on the research team we already have here,” Anastasis Germanidis, cofounder and Co-CEO at Runway. “We expect to expand further across Europe in the near future.” World models are AI systems that can learn from inputs such as audio, images, video and real-world data - in comparison to large language models (LLMs), which are primarily designed to understand and generate language rather than to model the physical.
